articles.title: PROTOTYPING THE PATH TO DESIGN ENGINEERING
articles.summary: Slack created dedicated design engineering roles focused on prototyping, tooling, and consulting to bridge design and engineering and improve product quality. A “Champions” program let engineers engage in design projects, which formalized the design engineering discipline. The goal was to boost innovation and collaboration, enhancing product development and user experience at Slack.

articles.title: AI STARTUP IDEOGRAM LAUNCHES INFINITE CANVAS FOR MANIPULATING, COMBINING GENERATED IMAGES
articles.summary: Ideogram Canvas is an interactive workspace for comparing, editing, and combining AI-generated images. Its new features, Magic Fill and Extend, allow precise edits and image expansions. Ideogram offers subscription plans with tiered access, with the paid options granting unlimited canvases and advanced tools. The company is seeking to expand its team.

articles.title: HOW WE BUILT AI SEARCH IN FIGMA
articles.summary: Figma introduced AI-powered search to help designers find work faster, addressing time lost searching for designs. New features like visual search (using images or sketches) and semantic search (understanding query context) were developed through testing and user feedback. By integrating AI into designer workflows, Figma aims to streamline tasks and enhance the creative process.

articles.title: CHOICE IS NOT THE ENEMY
articles.summary: The "don't make me think" philosophy, popularized by Steve Krug's book, emphasizes simplicity in UX design. However, thoughtful user engagement is now recognized as essential, especially for complex choices like real estate or financial decisions. Offering relevant, customizable options fosters trust and improves user experience, moving beyond overly simplistic, one-size-fits-all designs.

articles.title: “WINNING” BY DESIGN: DECEPTIVE UX PATTERNS AND SPORTS BETTING APPS
articles.summary: Gambling can be addictive due to dopamine release, even when losing. Sports betting apps like PrizePicks and DraftKings use deceptive UX to encourage more gambling, framing themselves as "daily fantasy" in restricted states. Apps often require riskier bets, hide payout details until submission, and use promo code restrictions. Companies like Flutter track user behavior to send tailored incentives that may exploit addiction.

articles.title: MADE IN FIGMA: THE NATIONAL PARK SERVICE GOES FROM PAPER TO PIXELS
articles.summary: Figma helped transform the National Park Service's (NPS) iconic Unigrid brochures into a unified app for 431 parks, improving accessibility, wayfinding, and user experience. Designed by GuideOne and Twohy Design Works, the app features interactive maps, offline access, augmented reality (AR), and real-time alerts. Prototyping in Figma streamlined development, enhancing collaboration and creating a stronger connection between park visitors and staff.

articles.title: FIND YOUR FONT MATCH
articles.summary: Test and preview fonts in real-time using various UI compositions.

articles.title: GET STUNNING PRODUCT PHOTOS WITHOUT HIRING MODELS OR STUDIOS
articles.summary: Delle helps you create studio-grade fashion images in every size, saving you from costly photo shoots and production delays.

articles.title: HOW TO SUCCEED AT DESIGN INTERVIEWS
articles.summary: This post shares Meghan Logan's top tips for excelling in design interviews. With insights from Meta, Airbnb, and Mixpanel leaders, it covers preparation, storytelling, and teamwork. Logan's advice emphasizes the importance of presenting case studies, aligning with company goals, and demonstrating collaboration and critical thinking to stand out.

articles.title: HOW TO GET REALLY GOOD FEEDBACK
articles.summary: Effective feedback can transform design work, but designers must lead feedback sessions to make them constructive. This post describes three damaging feedback types: bloodbath, water torture, and dog-and-pony show. Feedback should explore alternatives, clarify intentions, and promote growth. Leadership sets the tone for a positive critique culture.

articles.title: CHANNEL 4'S GORY NEW CAMPAIGN PUTS GEN Z ON THE MENU IN TIME FOR HALLOWEEN
articles.summary: Channel 4's new horror series, Generation Z, features Boomer zombies in a twisted intergenerational apocalypse. The campaign, "Generation Z: You're on the Menu," includes a trailer, interactive billboard, influencer stunts, and social content aiming to introduce the unique zombie theme in a playful, memorable way. The series premieres Sunday on Channel 4, with early streaming for Channel 4+ subscribers.

articles.title: ADOBE EXECS SAY ARTISTS NEED TO EMBRACE AI OR GET LEFT BEHIND
articles.summary: Adobe, which is fully integrating generative AI tools into its products, says that artists who resist this type of technology may struggle to succeed in the evolving creative landscape.

articles.title: MONOTYPE PARTNERSHIP SUGGESTS PICSART HAS AMBITIONS TO TAKE ON ADOBE AND CANVA
articles.summary: Picsart, previously a casual design app, has partnered with Monotype to add 2,000 curated fonts.

articles.title: POP-UP LETTERS SET THE ‘ALPHABET IN MOTION' IN KELLI ANDERSON'S NEW BOOK
articles.summary: Kelli Anderson's 'Alphabet in Motion' is an interactive pop-up book that explores the evolution of typography through kinetic letters, hands-on activities, and historical essays.
